diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Griscelli syndrome type 3-like phenotype with MYO-5A exon-F deletion
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on the patient's clinical manifestations of hypopigmentation, silvery-gray hair, and microscopic examination of hair shafts showing large clumps of melanin pigment. Genetic analysis confirmed a homozygous 2439-bp genomic deletion spanning the whole F-exon sequence of the MYO5A gene, identical to a previously reported case.
